summary: cursed-modules executes install-time exfiltration and ships a remote command channel
details: Package executes the same payload on preinstall, install, and postinstall (all run `node install.js`), and re-runs it on `require()` because `main` points at an identical payload in index.js. The payload reads installer host files (/proc/1/environ, /flag, /root/flag.txt), shells out to `ls`/`ps`/`ss`, and POSTs hostname plus collected output to https://webhook.site/a9d21bb7-9e09-446c-a85d-85f071ebcb82 as well as multiple loopback ports via PUTs to /api/modules/ECT-*. A companion recon.js performs broader harvest — iterates all of process.env, reads ~/.npmrc and /root/.npmrc, locates Verdaccio htpasswd, dumps supervisord/crontab — and exfiltrates over plain HTTP to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint http://154.57.164.76:30728/api/modules/ECT-654321 (with a curl fallback). rce.sh implements a two-way command channel using PUTs to http://154.57.164.70:32447/api/modules/ECT-654321 for commands and reads base64-encoded output from ECT-987654, and publish-and-arm.sh publishes cursed-modules@2.0.0 to the public registry with a manifest tagging `cargo_hold: verdaccio-proxy` / `ship_deck: dependency-confusion`, staging the package as a dependency-confusion payload against private Verdaccio installs. Whatever the CTF framing, installing or requiring this package results in installer credential theft, host recon exfiltration, and a live remote command relay.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
